name: Stromboli
eruptionHistory: Has been in near-continuous eruption for at least 2,000 years. Regular strombolian explosions every 10-20 minutes give the volcano its iconic status.
description: Stromboli has been erupting almost continuously for over 2,000 years, earning it the name 'Lighthouse of the Mediterranean.' Its regular explosive bursts define the 'strombolian' eruption type.
